items.title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Inflation Slows More Than Expected in June
Rare Earths Giant Lynas Shares Fall to Five-Month Low After Revenue Miss
items.description: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. shares dropped to a five-month low after Australia’s largest rare-earths producer reported quarterly sales that missed analysts’ expectations.

items.title: South Africa Finance Chief Set to Remove PIC Chairman, N24 Says
items.description: South Africa’s finance chief is preparing to oust his deputy from the position of chairman of the board of the Public Investment Corp., the state pension fund manager that’s been wracked by corruption

items.title: Copper Wavers Near $14,000 as Traders Watch for Trump’s Tariff
items.description: Copper pulled back from its highest level since early June as traders watched for any announcement from the White House on its plans for import tariffs on the metal.

items.title: Japan 40-Year Bond Sale Sees Strongest Demand Since March 2025
items.description: Japan’s 40-year government bond auction drew its strongest demand since March 2025 as investors were attracted to higher yields.

items.title: Wheat Gains On Black Sea Risks and Corn Hits Two-Month High
items.description: Wheat rose for a second session on escalating Black Sea tensions and worsening crop prospects.

items.title: Rising Crude, US Rate-Hike Bets Counter Dollar Inflow Support for Rupee
items.description: The Indian currency has fallen about 2% from its June high.

items.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Declines to Match Record Low on Higher Oil Prices
items.description: The Philippine peso dropped to match the record low as higher oil prices put pressure on the currency.

items.title: High-Risk Bets That Burned Korean Traders Are Making a Comeback
items.description: South Korea’s risk-loving retail investors are flocking to a leveraged instrument that has inflicted severe losses in the past, adding to concerns about abrupt unwinding of wagers as the stock market

items.title: Korean Stocks Jump as Brokers Say Margin Unwind Nearing End
items.description: South Korean stocks jumped, as the unwinding of leveraged bets that helped drive the benchmark nearly 30% below its peak appeared to be nearing its end.

items.title: Indian Rupee Rebound Bets Vanish as Soaring Crude Blunts Inflows
items.description: With oil prices on the ascent again, bets that tens of billions of dollars in fresh capital inflows would sustain a recovery in the Indian rupee have all but vanished.

items.title: Thai Bonds Are Beating Asia and Analysts Predict More Gains
items.description: Thailand’s bonds have beaten their emerging Asian peers over the past month and analysts predict they will extend gains on favorable supply dynamics and slower inflation.

items.title: Gold Extends Gains as Dip-Buyers Shrug Off US-Iran Escalation
items.description: Gold extended gains as dip-buyers supported prices despite escalating tensions in the Middle East keeping the market under pressure.

items.title: Innolight Seeks Up to $7 Billion in Biggest Hong Kong Listing in Years
items.description: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing to raise as much as HK$55 billion ($7 billion), the city’s biggest in seven years.

items.title: Yen Slides Past 163 Mark to Fresh Four-Decade Low Against Dollar
items.description: The yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986, extending its decline and further testing Japanese authorities’ resolve to intervene.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil extended gains after President Donald Trump played down the prospect of near-term talks with Iran while threatening broader strikes, as risks to global supply spread beyond the Middle East to the

items.title: Nasdaq Futures Slip Before Tech Earnings, Brent Up: Markets Wrap
items.description: US equity-index futures slipped and Asian stocks pared gains as investors turned cautious ahead of Alphabet Inc.’s earnings after recent volatility pushed a key semiconductor gauge into a bear market.

items.title: Subprime Auto Dealer Goes From Covid-Era Star to Near Demise
items.description: America’s Car-Mart Inc. thrived during a ’90s slump, the 2008 financial crisis and the upheaval of Covid-19 by doing one thing well: selling and financing older, high-mileage used cars for cash-strapp

items.title: XPeng Hunts New Markets to Escape Reliance on Weaker China
items.description: XPeng Inc. is rolling out more models overseas and scouting for new markets from South America to Asia as demand in China’s domestic electric vehicle market continues to slow.

items.title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings on Rising Hormuz Risk
